title: The Beggar's Quatrain.
content:
("Aveugle comme Hom�re.")

    [Improvised at the Caf� de Paris.]


    Blind, as was Homer; as Belisarius, blind,
    But one weak child to guide his vision dim.
    The hand which dealt him bread, in pity kind - 
    He'll never see; God sees it, though, for him.

    H.L.C., "London Society."
meaning:
This evocative piece by Victor-Marie Hugo, titled "The Beggar's Quatrain.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
